title: Capstone Case Study 1 - Phase 8 Project Closure, AMS Transition & Lessons Learned
learningObjectives: Transition SAP TM support to AMS

sections.title: Project Closure
sections.subtitle: Official Completion of SAP TM Implementation

sections.content: Thirty days after Go-Live, the SAP TM implementation has stabilized successfully. Business users are using the new transportation processes daily, critical incidents have been resolved and the Steering Committee has scheduled the final project closure meeting.

notes: Daily Hypercare meetings reduced incident resolution time.
Standard SAP functionality reduced customization effort.

sections.background: The CIO asks whether the SAP TM implementation achieved its original objectives and whether the organization should invest in additional SAP TM capabilities such as AI-based optimization and predictive transportation planning.
questions: Did SAP TM deliver measurable business value?
What improvements should be planned next?
What risks remain after project closure?

sections.title: Future Roadmap
sections.question: Which recommendation should be presented to the Steering Committee?

options.text: Close the project and make no further improvements.
Continue with a Phase-2 roadmap including AI optimization, advanced analytics, carrier collaboration and continuous process improvement.
Replace SAP TM with custom development.

sections.explanation: Successful SAP implementations typically continue with optimization phases that increase business value after the initial Go-Live has stabilized.

sections.title: Solution Architect Advice
sections.content: Project closure is not the end of the SAP journey. A successful implementation establishes a stable foundation for continuous optimization, innovation and business transformation.

sections.title: Real SAP Project
sections.content: Many organizations begin Phase-2 improvements within six months of Go-Live, focusing on predictive analytics, AI-assisted transportation planning, sustainability metrics, carrier collaboration portals and transportation network optimization.

sections.title: Accenture Project Closure Question
sections.content: How do you determine whether an SAP TM implementation was successful? A consultant should evaluate business KPIs, user adoption, production stability, budget, schedule, executive satisfaction and measurable business benefits rather than simply confirming that the system went live.

sections.title: Capstone Completed
sections.content: The SAP TM implementation has been successfully completed from project initiation through production stabilization and AMS transition. The organization has achieved measurable business improvements and established a roadmap for future transportation innovation.
